agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The domain spans appointment scheduling, physician credentialing, medical records access control, and clinical documentation lifecycle. Subject to healthcare compliance requirements around data privacy and practitioner licensing.","bounded_context":"Appointment & Consultation Management — covers the lifecycle from booking through consultation completion and note finalization, intersecting with Physician Credentialing and Patient Records access control.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled time slot binding exactly one patient to exactly one licensed physician for a virtual consultation. Has a start time, end time, and lifecycle states (scheduled, in-progress, completed, cancelled)."},{"term":"Licensed Physician","definition":"A medical practitioner whose professional license number has been verified by the platform and who is therefore eligible to accept patient bookings."},{"term":"Double-Booking","definition":"A scheduling conflict where two or more appointments for the same physician have overlapping time ranges. This is a forbidden state."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period ending 2 hours before an appointment's start time, during which the patient retains self-service cancellation rights."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"A platform operator with elevated privileges, including the ability to cancel appointments outside the patient cancellation window."},{"term":"Treating Physician","definition":"The specific physician assigned to a given appointment, and thereby granted access to that patient's medical records for the scope of treatment."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical documentation created by the treating physician during or after an appointment. Subject to a time-based locking policy."},{"term":"Note Lock","definition":"An irreversible transition of consultation notes to a read-only state, triggered 48 hours after the associated appointment ends. Once locked, notes cannot be edited by anyone."},{"term":"License Verification","definition":"The process of validating a physician's professional license number before the platform permits them to accept bookings. A physician without verified status is ineligible for scheduling."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient may only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified by the platform.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing bookings with unverified practitioners would violate healthcare licensing regulations and expose patients to unqualified providers. This is a compliance and safety constraint that cannot be relaxed by policy.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create an appointment referencing a physician whose license_verified status is false MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments with overlapping time ranges. No two appointments for the same physician may share any point in time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking corrupts scheduling integrity — a physician cannot physically attend two consultations simultaneously. Violating this makes the schedule data meaningless.","testable_assertion":"Given physician P has appointment A from T1 to T2, any new appointment for P where new_start < T2 AND new_end > T1 MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their own appointment only if the current time is at least 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that balances patient flexibility against physician schedule stability. Management could adjust this window (e.g., to 1 hour or 4 hours) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A patient-initiated cancellation request MUST be rejected if current_time > (appointment_start_time - 2 hours)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A staff administrator may cancel any appointment regardless of how close it is to the start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational policy granting override capability to staff. The scope of admin cancellation rights is a policy decision.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request from a staff administrator MUST succeed regardless of the time remaining before appointment start."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Patient medical records are visible only to the patient themselves and their treating physician.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data privacy and compliance constraint (aligned with HIPAA-type regulations). Unauthorized access to medical records is a regulatory violation and a fundamental breach of patient trust. Cannot be relaxed by policy.","testable_assertion":"Any request to access patient medical records MUST be denied unless the requester is the patient OR the physician assigned to an appointment with that patient."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Consultation notes become permanently read-only 48 hours after the associated appointment ends.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 48-hour window is a policy choice balancing physician flexibility to amend notes against the need for record integrity. The timeframe could be adjusted by compliance policy without corrupting the system.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to modify consultation notes where current_time > (appointment_end_time + 48 hours) MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A physician must have a verified license number before they can appear as bookable on the platform.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is the credentialing gate. Without it, the booking invariant (BR-1) cannot be enforced. Verification is a precondition for entering the schedulable physician pool.","testable_assertion":"A physician record with license_verified = false MUST NOT appear in any patient-facing booking search or availability query."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A physician with a verified license and available time slot","when":"A patient books an appointment within that available slot","then":"The appointment is created successfully, the time slot is reserved, and no overlapping booking is possible for that physician"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A physician whose license has NOT been verified","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with that physician","then":"The booking is rejected with a clear error indicating the physician is not currently available for appointments"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A physician with an existing appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"Another patient attempts to book that physician for 10:15 to 10:45","then":"The booking is rejected due to a scheduling conflict with the existing appointment"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A patient with an appointment scheduled at 15:00 and the current time is 12:00 (3 hours before)","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully since 3 hours > 2-hour cancellation window"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient with an appointment scheduled at 15:00 and the current time is 13:30 (1.5 hours before)","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation is rejected because 1.5 hours < 2-hour cancellation window"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An appointment scheduled at 15:00 and the current time is 14:45 (15 minutes before)","when":"A staff administrator requests cancellation","then":"The appointment is cancelled successfully regardless of the time proximity"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A patient with medical records and an appointment with Dr. Smith","when":"Dr. Smith requests access to the patient's medical records","then":"Access is granted because Dr. Smith is the treating physician for that patient"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A patient with medical records and no appointment with Dr. Jones","when":"Dr. Jones requests access to the patient's medical records","then":"Access is denied because Dr. Jones is not a treating physician for that patient"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A patient viewing their own profile","when":"The patient requests access to their own medical records","then":"Access is granted"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An appointment that ended at 14:00 on Monday and consultation notes were written","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit notes at 13:00 on Wednesday (47 hours later)","then":"The edit is permitted because the 48-hour window has not yet elapsed"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An appointment that ended at 14:00 on Monday and consultation notes were written","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit notes at 15:00 on Wednesday (49 hours later)","then":"The edit is rejected because the 48-hour lock window has passed and notes are now read-only"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A physician with an unverified license","when":"A patient searches for available physicians","then":"The unverified physician does not appear in the search results"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Appointment exactly at the 2-hour cancellation boundary (current time = start - 2 hours precisely)","expected_behavior":"The cancellation should be permitted. The rule states 'up to 2 hours before,' meaning at exactly 2 hours the patient still has the right to cancel. The cutoff is strictly less than 2 hours remaining."},{"scenario":"Physician's license is revoked or expires after appointments have been booked but before they occur","expected_behavior":"Existing future appointments should be flagged for review or cancelled by staff administrators. The physician should be immediately removed from the bookable pool. Patients should be notified and offered rebooking."},{"scenario":"Back-to-back appointments where one ends at exactly the same time another starts (e.g., 10:00-10:30 and 10:30-11:00)","expected_behavior":"This is NOT a double-booking. The appointments are adjacent but not overlapping. The overlap check uses strict inequality: new_start < existing_end AND new_end > existing_start. Sharing only a boundary point does not overlap."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes edited at exactly the 48-hour boundary","expected_behavior":"An edit submitted at exactly 48 hours post-appointment-end should be rejected. The lock activates at the 48-hour mark. The editable window is strictly less than 48 hours after end."},{"scenario":"Patient cancels and immediately rebooks the same slot","expected_behavior":"The cancellation frees the slot. The rebooking should succeed if the slot is still within the cancellation window and the physician has no other conflicting booking that was created in between."},{"scenario":"A physician is both a patient in the system and a treating physician for another patient","expected_behavior":"Roles are context-dependent. As a patient, they see their own records. As a treating physician, they see their assigned patients' records. No cross-contamination of access."},{"scenario":"Staff administrator attempts to view patient medical records","expected_behavior":"Unless the spec explicitly grants administrators access to medical records, they should be denied. BR-5 restricts visibility to the patient and treating physician only. Admin cancellation rights do not imply records access."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes for a cancelled appointment","expected_behavior":"If notes were created before cancellation, the 48-hour lock timer should still apply based on the cancellation time (or original end time, whichever is applicable). If no consultation occurred, notes should not exist."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect double-booking wastes physician time and creates patient dissatisfaction leading to churn. License verification failures expose the platform to malpractice liability and regulatory fines. Note-locking failures could lead to legal disputes over altered records.","operational":"The double-booking invariant is the backbone of scheduling efficiency. The cancellation policy balances physician utilization (reducing no-shows) against patient flexibility. The 48-hour note lock creates a clear audit trail for clinical documentation.","user":"Patients need confidence that they are seeing verified physicians and that their records are private. The 2-hour cancellation window provides reasonable flexibility while respecting physician schedules. Physicians need trust that their schedules are conflict-free.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) HIPAA-style violations if medical records access control is misconfigured — regulatory fines and reputational damage. (2) Unverified physicians accepting bookings — malpractice exposure. (3) Consultation notes modified after lock window — legal and audit trail integrity compromised.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Directly affected by booking integrity, cancellation flexibility, records privacy, and assurance of physician licensing."},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Depend on conflict-free scheduling, appropriate records access for treatment, and a reasonable window to finalize consultation notes."},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Need override cancellation capability for operational flexibility; must understand they do NOT have medical records access."},{"group":"Compliance/Legal Team","impact":"Rely on license verification, records access control, and note locking to meet regulatory obligations."},{"group":"Platform Operator","impact":"Business viability depends on trust — scheduling integrity, privacy, and credentialing are foundational to the platform's value proposition."}]},"confidence":{"level":88,"reasoning":"The specification is clear, explicit, and internally consistent. All six rules are well-defined with specific thresholds and clear actor roles. No contradictions detected. Minor boundary-condition ambiguities exist (exact boundary behavior for the 2-hour and 48-hour windows) but these are standard implementation details, not specification defects.","high_confidence_areas":["Double-booking prevention — unambiguously defined","License verification as a precondition for bookability","Role-based cancellation rights with clear time boundary","Medical records access restricted to patient and treating physician","Consultation notes locking after 48 hours"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether 'treating physician' access persists after the appointment ends or is scoped to active treatment only","Whether staff administrators have any medical records access for operational purposes","Behavior when a physician's license is revoked mid-schedule (not addressed in spec)","Whether the 48-hour note lock is computed from appointment end time or consultation completion time","Cancellation policy for no-shows or missed appointments"],"assumptions":["The 2-hour cancellation boundary is inclusive (patient can cancel at exactly 2 hours before)","Back-to-back appointments sharing only a boundary timestamp are permitted (not considered overlapping)","The 48-hour note lock is irreversible — no override mechanism exists","Treating physician access to records is scoped to patients with whom they have appointments","Staff administrator cancellation rights do not extend to medical records access"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The domain spans appointment scheduling, physician credentialing, medical records access control, and clinical documentation lifecycle. Subject to healthcare compliance requirements around data privacy and practitioner licensing.","bounded_context":"Appointment & Consultation Management — covers the lifecycle from booking through consultation completion and note

content.thinking:
This is a straightforward domain analysis for a telemedicine platform. The rules are clear and well-specified. Let me analyze and produce the JSON output.

No contradictions, no ambiguity issues, no missing context that would block analysis. The rules are clear enough to produce high-confidence output.

Let me classify business rules as invariants or not:
1. Licensed physician requirement - invariant (data integrity)
2. No double-booking - invariant (scheduling integrity)
3. Cancellation policy (2hr window) - policy, not invariant
4. License verification before bookings - invariant (compliance)
5. Medical records visibility - invariant (privacy/compliance)


6. Consultation notes locked after 48hrs - policy (could be changed)

The spec is clear and well-defined with high confidence. No escalations needed.
